description:
GOAL: Optimize hot calls by fusing adjacent frames into one Cranelift fn — no
allocation for fused calls. Performance-load-bearing for v1.

## Decisions captured

- IN SCOPE for v1, NOT deferred (user decision: "i think we should inline").
  Naive frame-per-call is too slow for tight loops.
- Operate on fz-IR BEFORE Cranelift lowering. Module-level pass: in-place
  rewrites of caller FnIrs. Produces a "merged" caller whose body absorbs the
  callee's blocks (renumbered) directly.
- Static decision (NOT tier-up-driven). Decided once at codegen time.
- New module: src/ir_inline.rs. Public entry: `pub fn inline_module(m: &mut Module)`.
- Pipeline placement: inside compile_with_backend, AFTER
  rewrite_known_target_closures (ir_codegen.rs:1907), BEFORE the final
  type_module call (ir_codegen.rs:1908). This is later than the original
  "before ir_typer" framing — rewrite_known_target_closures runs first and
  demotes known-target CallClosure/TailCallClosure to Call/TailCall, so the
  inliner sees direct-call shapes only. The final type_module still runs after
  inlining, so the typer sees fused bodies (no regression in dispatch-elision
  opportunity).
- v0 call-shape scope: TailCall and Call+Cont only (user decision 2026-05-11;
  closure-call cases eliminated 2026-05-14). Closure-call inlining (the
  original Cases 3-4) is eliminated: rewrite_known_target_closures
  (fz-ul4.29.10.3) already converts known-target CallClosure/TailCallClosure
  to direct Call/TailCall before the inliner runs. Remaining
  CallClosure/TailCallClosure are dynamically dispatched and cannot be
  statically inlined.
- v0 heuristic: leaf callees only, <= 8 IR stmts (user decision 2026-05-11).
  Single-callsite-anywhere heuristic is NOT in v0.

## Architecture (researched 2026-05-11, updated 2026-05-14 against actual IR)

fz-IR is in CPS form (see src/fz_ir.rs). Two callsite shapes are in scope:

  1. Term::TailCall { callee: FnId, args }
       Caller forwards its continuation; callee receives our cont_ptr.
       Runtime (emit_tail_call, ir_codegen.rs:5320): if self_id == callee_id,
       overwrite frame slots in place; else alloc fresh.

  2. Term::Call { callee: FnId, args, continuation: Cont { fn_id, captured } }
       Allocates a NEW continuation frame (emit_call, ir_codegen.rs:5171),
       runs callee. Callee's Return writes result into cont_frame[24] (cont's
       first param slot) and returns cont_ptr; trampoline drives the cont.

NOT in scope for the inliner:
- Term::CallClosure / TailCallClosure: known-target ones are already demoted
  by rewrite_known_target_closures; the remainder are dynamic.
- Term::Receive: scheduler yield point. A fn whose body contains Receive MUST
  retain a real frame for process suspension/resume. Inlining such a fn as a
  callee would corrupt the continuation chain.

Continuations are themselves real FnIrs in the module — produced by
cps_split_call (ir_lower.rs:1039) and cps_split_call_closure
(ir_lower.rs:929). A continuation's entry block params are [result_param,
cap_params...] — result FIRST, then captured.

Lambda entry params: [captured..., callsite_args...] (ir_lower.rs:887-892).

## Inlining semantics by case

  Case 1 (TailCall):
    - Renumber callee's blocks and vars (alpha_rename).
    - Caller's `TailCall(F, args)` becomes `Goto(F_entry', args)`.
    - F's `Return(v)` stays `Return(v')` (caller's cont is unchanged).
    - F's `Halt(v)` stays `Halt(v')`.

  Case 2 (Call+Cont):
    - Renumber.
    - Caller's `Call(F, args) -> Cont{c, captured}` becomes
      `Goto(F_entry', args)`.
    - F's `Return(v)` becomes `TailCall(c, [v', captured...])`. The
      continuation is a real FnIr; calling it as TailCall is operationally
      identical to the runtime's cont mechanism (ir_interp.rs:231-245).
    - F's `Halt(v)` stays `Halt(v')`.

## Why "leaf" is the right v0 heuristic

A leaf fn has only Goto/If/Return/Halt terminators — NO Call/TailCall/
CallClosure/TailCallClosure/Receive inside its body. Leaf-only inlining:

  - Cannot reintroduce recursion (cycles must traverse a Call/TailCall term).
    Direct self-recursion (callee.id == caller.id) is also defended against.
  - Doesn't pull in dependent continuations or nested closures.
  - Term::Receive explicitly excluded: inlining a fn that suspends would
    corrupt the process's continuation chain (the yield expects a real
    frame for resume).
  - Keeps frame schemas valid: ir_codegen builds schemas from each fn's entry
    param count (ir_codegen.rs:745-763). Inlining doesn't change the caller's
    entry params, so the caller's schema is unaffected. The callee's schema is
    also untouched; if other callsites remain, the callee is still compiled
    normally.
  - The introduced post-inline Return->TailCall (Case 2) adds a new TailCall
    to the caller, but to an already-existing continuation FnIr — no new fns
    are created, no schemas change.

## Frame schema interaction

ir_codegen builds frame schemas from entry-block param count
(ir_codegen.rs:745-763). Schemas are per-fn; inlining does not change any
fn's entry params, so no schema recomputation is needed.

Note: ir_liveness.rs was deleted in fz-ul4.11.30. The comment in fz_ir.rs
about "liveness populating frame_schema_id" is stale documentation.

## SourceInfo side-table

Module.source (SourceInfo) maps Var.0 to source spans and names. Alpha-rename
mints new Vars; those vars will have no SourceInfo entries (DUMMY spans). This
is acceptable — ir_typer uses SourceInfo for diagnostics only, not for
correctness. No action required in the inliner.

## Configuration

  pub const INLINE_BUDGET: usize = 8;     // total Stmt::Let cap on callee
  pub const MAX_ITERATIONS: usize = 3;    // fixpoint cap
  pub const GROWTH_CAP: usize = 4;        // per-fn post-inline <= 4x original

User-decided 2026-05-11. Consts in ir_inline.rs, not runtime knobs.

## Children (in dependency order)

  - fz-ul4.11.15.1 — Inliner skeleton + leaf detector + alpha-rename primitives
  - fz-ul4.11.15.2 — TailCall inlining (Case 1)
  - fz-ul4.11.15.3 — Call+Cont inlining (Case 2)
  - fz-ul4.11.15.5 — Fixpoint + bloat cap + pipeline integration
  - fz-ul4.11.15.6 — Hot-loop assert + parity + ticket closeout

(fz-ul4.11.15.4 closed: closure-call inlining eliminated; handled upstream
by rewrite_known_target_closures in fz-ul4.29.10.3.)

## Tests (rolled up across children)

  - Hot loops show no per-iter allocation after inlining.
  - GC still finds correct roots in inlined code.
  - Inliner doesn't introduce code bloat beyond GROWTH_CAP per fn.
  - ir_interp parity: inlined module produces same values as un-inlined module.

## Risks

  - Inliner correctness: must not violate liveness invariants or frame
    layouts. Mitigation: ir_interp parity test on every change.
  - Inliner too aggressive: code bloat -> instruction cache pressure.
    Mitigation: GROWTH_CAP + MAX_ITERATIONS.
  - Forgetting to remap a nested Var (e.g., BitFieldIr::size = BitSizeIr::
    Var(v) inside MakeBitstring/BitReadField). Mitigation: exhaustive Prim
    walk in alpha_rename; targeted test per Prim variant.

## Deferred (tracked as explicit follow-up tickets)

  - fz-ul4.11.26 — @no_inline attribute (per-fn opt-out)
  - fz-ul4.11.27 — Cross-module inlining
  - fz-ul4.11.28 — Bounded recursion unrolling
  - fz-ul4.11.29 — Dead-callee DCE pass

## References

  - src/fz_ir.rs:190-239   — Prim variants (incl. BitFieldIr nested Vars)
  - src/fz_ir.rs:254-293   — Term variants (Receive at 291-293)
  - src/fz_ir.rs:248-252   — Cont
  - src/fz_ir.rs:304-321   — FnIr, Module
  - src/ir_lower.rs:1039   — cps_split_call
  - src/ir_lower.rs:929    — cps_split_call_closure
  - src/ir_lower.rs:868    — lower_lambda (entry params: [captured..., args...])
  - src/ir_codegen.rs:5171 — emit_call
  - src/ir_codegen.rs:5320 — emit_tail_call
  - src/ir_codegen.rs:745  — build_frame_schema
  - src/ir_codegen.rs:1896-1908 — pipeline: typer runs, rewrite passes
  - src/ir_interp.rs:231   — call/return semantics (Term::Call branch)
  - src/ir_interp.rs:364   — unpack_closure (fz-ul4.29.5 layout)
title: Frame inliner (performance optimization)
